agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Higher fundraising efficiency reflects productive local donor markets or agglomeration dynamics, which correlate with higher absolute counts of human services.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Okten & Weisbrod (2000) study fundraising returns and demand-side variables.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A statistically significant positive correlation was found, though extremely weak. Areas with higher nonprofit fundraising efficiency have a tiny increase in the absolute count of social services.
